task: I need to send a short follow-up to a prospect who clicked an email but didn’t book a call. Can you help me draft something that sounds professional, gets to the point fast, and gives them a clear next step without sounding pushy?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, professional follow-up you can use:

**Subject:** Quick follow-up

Hi [Name],  

Thanks for taking a look at my email. I wanted to follow up in case this is still a priority for you. If it’s helpful, I’d be happy to share a bit more or set up a quick call to see if it makes sense to continue the conversation.  

If now isn’t the right time, no worries at all — just let me know.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **warmer version**
- a **more direct sales version**
- or a **super short 2–3 sentence version**

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but this is a little too soft. I’d want it tighter and more action-oriented. Right now it kind of asks permission twice.

Can you give me 2-3 versions that are:
- short
- professional
- clear next step
- not cheesy

Also, make one version a little more direct since the email already got the click.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 3 tighter versions with a clearer next step:

### 1) Professional and concise
**Subject:** Following up

Hi [Name],  

Thanks for taking a look. If this is still a priority, I’d suggest we set up a quick call to see whether it makes sense to move forward. You can grab a time here: [link].  

Best,  
[Your Name]

### 2) Slightly more direct
**Subject:** Next step

Hi [Name],  

I saw you checked out my note, so I wanted to follow up directly. If you’re open to it, let’s get a quick call on the calendar and see if there’s a fit. Here’s my link: [link].  

Best,  
[Your Name]

### 3) Very short / clean
**Subject:** Quick follow-up

Hi [Name],  

Following up here — if this is still worth exploring, please book a time here: [link]. Happy to continue the conversation.  

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make these:
- **more executive / polished**
- **more salesy**
- or **even shorter and sharper**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is solid. The second one is probably the best fit — direct without sounding goofy.

If I were tightening it a bit more, I’d probably use:

**Subject:** Next step

Hi [Name],

Saw you checked it out, so I wanted to follow up directly. If it’s still relevant, let’s get a quick call on the calendar and see if it makes sense to move forward. Here’s my link: [link].

Best,
[Your Name]

The first one works too, just a little softer. Third one is fine if you want it super short.
